title: The Lily And The Rose.
content:
آ آ آ آ The nymph must lose her female friend,
آ آ آ آ If more admired than sheï؟½
آ آ آ آ But where will fierce contention end,
آ آ آ آ If flowers can disagree?


آ آ آ آ Within the gardenï؟½s peaceful scene
آ آ آ آ Appearï؟½d two lovely foes,
آ آ آ آ Aspiring to the rank of queen,
آ آ آ آ The Lily and the Rose.


آ آ آ آ The Rose soon reddenï؟½d into rage,
آ آ آ آ And, swelling with disdain,
آ آ آ آ Appealï؟½d to many a poetï؟½s page
آ آ آ آ To prove her right to reign.


آ آ آ آ The Lilyï؟½s height bespoke command,
آ آ آ آ A fair imperial flower;
آ آ آ آ She seemï؟½d designï؟½d for Floraï؟½s hand,
آ آ آ آ The sceptre of her power.


آ آ آ آ This civil bickering and debate
آ آ آ آ The goddess chanced to hear,
آ آ آ آ And flew to save, ere yet too late,
آ آ آ آ The pride of the parterre.


آ آ آ آ Yours is, she said, the nobler hue,
آ آ آ آ And yours the statelier mien;
آ آ آ آ And, till a third surpasses you,
آ آ آ آ Let each be deemï؟½d a queen.


آ آ آ آ Thus soothed and reconciled, each seeks
آ آ آ آ The fairest British fair;
آ آ آ آ The seat of empire is her cheeks,
آ آ آ آ They reign united there.
meaning:
William Cowper's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Lily And The Rose."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
